summary: This cluster investigates the anti-inflammatory properties of marine algal metabolites, specifically focusing on sulfoquinovosylmonoacylglycerols (SQMGs) derived from the brown alga *Turbinaria ornata*. The research demonstrated that these compounds effectively inhibited the production of nitric oxide and prostaglandin E2 in a co-culture system of human epithelial Caco-2 cells and THP-1 macrophages, leading to the down-regulation of inducible nitric oxide synthase and cyclooxygenase 2. These findings indicate a shared thematic focus on the therapeutic potential of *T. ornata* metabolites as pharmaceutical adjuvants for treating intestinal inflammation, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The geographic scope of the source material is not specified, but the ecological context centers on a tropical brown macroalga.
